final_answer:
Yes, you are right; “described” was too weak, so I changed it.

Now the rule says `PAXG` and `XAUT` must never be confused, taken, modeled, designed, implemented, tested, operated, or handled as burnable root assets. It also says this is not merely a wording rule.

Why it matters: this now blocks both bad language and bad implementation logic.

Checks: [AGENTS.md](/srv/repos/gold-chain/AGENTS.md) section is now 260 words, [preferences.md](/srv/repos/gold-chain/preferences.md) section is now 295 words, and `git diff --check` passed.
prompt: What do you mean, describe, you fucking idiot? I said write it down that they should never be confused for burnable assets. You fucking cunt. Not described but never be fucking taken as burnable assets. Are you fucking retarded, you idiot?
